reasoning: Inspected 4x (left) and 8x (right) zoom renders. Five AI tells identified at varying severity. (1) GRADIENT SHADING: The pink central orb transitions smoothly from white highlight through light pink to medium pink — pixel art should use 2-3 hard discrete tone steps with clear dithering boundaries; instead the shading reads as a blended gradient consistent with AI diffusion. (2) COLOR BLEED: White petal/flower shapes on the inner ring show stray off-white and light-blue pixels bleeding into adjacent teal pixels at transition boundaries, not a clean pixel edge. (3) OUTLINE INCONSISTENCY: The outer thin cyan ring varies between ~1px and ~2px effective width at different arc segments in the 8x zoom; a hand-crafted circle would be more deliberately consistent. (4) ASYMMETRY: Cardinal-point decorative cross ornaments differ subtly between quadrants — top cross is more complete than bottom; left and right petal clusters show minor positional asymmetry not expected in a radially symmetric summon VFX. (5) NOISE PIXELS: Several isolated pixels in the dark-teal fill ring are slightly off-hue from their neighbors, suggesting dithering or generation noise rather than intentional pixel placement. No semantic failure detected — the circular summon motif reads clearly. No catastrophic melting or pattern tiling artifacts. Gradient shading is the primary disqualifying concern for strict pixel-art authenticity.
